title: The Rise of Big Data
summary: When data outgrew single-machine systems, distributed computing became practical: spread storage and processing across many machines. This shift helped launch the big-data era and the data lake model.

context.headline: Too big for one machine — so use a thousand
context.description: As data grew in volume, velocity, and variety, scaling up one machine reached economic and technical limits. The breakthrough was distributed computing: spread storage and processing across many machines. Combined with low-cost cloud object storage, this enabled data lakes - systems that store raw data first and apply structure later when needed.
context.when: From Google's landmark papers to the cloud data lake
